# truenas_pool (Data Source)

Reads a TrueNAS storage pool by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "truenas_pool" "tank" {
  name = "tank"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the pool.

### Read-Only

- `allocated` (Number) Allocated pool space in bytes.
- `autotrim` (String) Autotrim setting (`on` or `off`).
- `dedup_table_quota` (String) Quota setting for the deduplication table.
- `dedup_table_size` (Number) Size of the deduplication table in bytes.
- `fragmentation` (String) Pool fragmentation percentage as a string.
- `free` (Number) Free pool space in bytes.
- `freeing` (Number) Space being freed in bytes.
- `guid` (String) Globally unique identifier of the pool.
- `healthy` (Boolean) Whether the pool is healthy.
- `id` (Number) Internal TrueNAS API identifier of the pool.
- `is_upgraded` (Boolean) Whether the pool has been upgraded to the latest ZFS feature flags.
- `path` (String) Mount path of the pool.
- `scan` (Attributes) Details of the last pool scan (scrub). Null if no scan has run. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--scan))
- `size` (Number) Total pool capacity in bytes.
- `status` (String) Current status of the pool (e.g. `ONLINE`, `DEGRADED`).
- `status_code` (String) Machine-readable pool status code.
- `status_detail` (String) Human-readable pool status detail.
- `warning` (Boolean) Whether the pool has warnings.

<a id="nestedatt--scan"></a>
### Nested Schema for `scan`

Read-Only:

- `bytes_issued` (Number) Bytes issued so far.
- `bytes_processed` (Number) Bytes processed so far.
- `bytes_to_process` (Number) Total bytes to process.
- `end_time` (String) Scan end time in RFC 3339 format.
- `errors` (Number) Number of scan errors.
- `function` (String) Type of scan (e.g. `SCRUB`).
- `pause` (String) Time the scan was paused, in RFC 3339 format. Null if not paused.
- `percentage` (Number) Percentage of scan completed.
- `start_time` (String) Scan start time in RFC 3339 format.
- `state` (String) State of the scan (e.g. `FINISHED`, `SCANNING`).
- `total_secs_left` (Number) Estimated seconds remaining in the scan. Null if not scanning.
